channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NZXT To Pay $3.45 Million Settlement Over PC Rental Program
item.content:encoded:
NZXT, the company known for its pre-built PCs and other components, and its partner Fragile have agreed to pay a $3.45 million settlement for a class-action lawsuit that was brought against the companies.

The lawsuit claimed that NZXT’s Flex PC rental program was designed to “scam” customers who were looking for a modern gaming PC.

Announced in July 2024, the system was met with harsh criticism almost immediately. The noise became so loud that NZXT’s CEO publicly responded to customer concerns regarding rent-to-own, parts used in the machines, and more. That wasn’t enough, however, as three customers filed a complaint in August 2025 against the company.

The lawsuit alleged that NZXT and Fragile, the company’s payment partner, participated in “bait-and-switch” mechanics to defraud customers. The lawsuit alleges:

Defendants accomplished their PC rental scheme through a bait-and-switch where Defendants (1) advertised PCs with specific components and attributes but provided consumers with lower quality components PCs and (2) misrepresented the nature of the Program including that there were no contracts, no cancellation fees, that consumers would own the PC, and leading customers to reasonably believe the Program was a rent-to-own agreement.

On April 7, first spotted by Gamers Nexus, a settlement was reached with a group of 19,322 NZXT customers. This settlement, which is pending judge approval, would include $923,117 of debt forgiveness that provides up to $5,000 to those who are 90 days past due on payments.

In addition to the debt forgiveness, some customers will also be able to keep and own their PCs if they meet the requirements, such as paying into the program for two years or more.

Finally, a $1.45 million settlement cash fund is also being allocated to payout to customers. For those who aren’t eligible for debt relief or the retention plan can fill out a claim form—available after settlement approval—to receive cash relief.

Did you ever sign up for the NZXT Flex PC rental program, and what do you think of the $3.45 million settlement? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Take-Two Interactive gaining $1 billion in market value after the recent GTA leak. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: League of Legends Gets WASD in Ranked and Big Arena Update
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released another Dev Update for League of Legends to kick off Season 2 Act 1: Pandemonium.

With no big mid-season update for LoL this year, Season 2 2026 will be shorter than usual, lasting for six patches rather than the usual eight, to make room for a bigger Season 3 later this year.

Yet, Riot has announced a ton of new changes coming to LoL in the upcoming months.

For example, games will now auto-detect trolling behaviour and if so, will allow teams an earlier surrender vote, much like how AFK detection works. The inting player will not get to participate in the vote and will be punished, while their teammates keep their LP and the opponents receive full LP for a win.

This comes along with WASD in ranked, Discord integration, a massive overhaul for Arena and more revealed for Season 2.

Starting with Patch 26.9, Riot will controversially allow players to use WASD controls in their ranked LoL matches.

The control scheme was first released in unranked queues to test for bugs and balance and Riot now believes it’s in a healthy enough state for ranked.

Riot claims that WASD controllers still have a slightly worse win rate than traditional point and click players but believes the difference between the two will decrease as players master WASD controls.

And in a huge change that benefits both types of players, Riot is allowing players to create different keybinds for each champion.

This means that players can toggle on and off Smart Cast on certain champions, which is particularly huge for Rumble and Vikor mains.

And it’ll also mean that players can use WASD controls on only champions they think it benefits, such as ADCs as WASD makes kiting much easier.

New Skins

Due to the shorter season, the battle pass will be quicker to complete this time around and the non-prestige skins will be purchasable directly in the store.

New prestige skins are coming for Shaco and LeBlanc this season and for Veigar next season. As well as the ‘Pandemonium’ skins revealed in the gameplay trailer, new skins in Rain Shepherd Ivern and PROJECT: Quinn are coming soon.

There will also be new Day Jobs skins with a cooking theme in Breadsticks Irelia and Sphaghetti alla Vel’Koz.

Gameplay Changes

Riot has updated Role Quests with more forgiving rules to allow roam-heavy champions to still complete their bonuses as long as they still visit their lane occasionally.

Additionally, top laners will now receive more Role Quest experience for teamfighting and, instead of an empowered recall, midlaners will now be rewarded with 6% bonus AP and AD for completing their Quest.

These changes are to ensure that the solo lane Quests benefit a wider variety of champions rather than just split pushers in the top lane and non-roaming mids.

Riot has also promised item adjustments and new Keystones to help create more diverse build options for a lot of champions, providing examples such as AP Ezreal and Attack Speed Xin Zhao as the sort of alternatives build paths the publisher wants to support.

This is leading to the return of the runes Deathfire Touch and Stormraider’s Stormsurge to the Sorcery tree, with the latter replacing Phase Rush.

Season 2 will also see item adjustments, particularly for Dusk and Dawn, as well as two new starting items, new Omnivamp Boots, and the removal of both Trailblazer and Opportunity.

New Champion Locke

The next new LoL champion was talked about by Riot for the first time too. While they didn’t want to name the character, we already know Locke is League’s next champion due to an April Fools’ joke.

Riot confirmed that Locke will be an AP Assassin intended for the mid lane and will release in Act 2 of Season 2.

Riot is adding big updates to Arena mode.

Arena will now feature events which mix things up, with three events planned for Season 2, starting with LoL Patch 26.10.

The first Arena event will last for three patches and will change the mode to six teams of three, increasing the size of teams while decreasing the number of teams.

The second event will opt every player into Bravery, the feature that has players play a random champion rather than one they selected in order to get additional bonuses. But instead of full Bravery, players will have a small pool to choose from.

The third and final Arena event of Season 2 will be Swift Arena, making matches faster with just four teams.

Additionally, Arena is receiving a new map called Petricite Grove. Based on the outskirts of Demacia, just like the new Pandemonium theme for the season, the map has lots of walls in tribute to Season 2 star Vayne, as well as bombs scattered around the map which players can send rolling before detonating.

Another new Arena Feature is Augment Levels, allowing players to level up their favourite Augments. After levelling up two Augments, players can use future Augment choices to upgrade these Augments rather than adding new ones.

Riot has also promised that while a player can only have four Augments at a time, Augments will be offered more frequently.

And Riot is also adding 20 reworked Guests of Honor to the mode, including Nocturne who will create dark zones in maps and Shaco who will make Augments roll random tiers.

There will also be 30 new Augments added to the mode, some from Mayhem and some totally new.

Finally, Riot has announced that Discord will be integrated into the League client, starting with a beta in USA, Canada, and Brazil.

Players will be able to link Discord and Riot accounts in order to invite Discord friends to in-game parties without having to leave the client.

Players will be able to copy and paste party codes in order to mass invite friends rather than one at a time.

This is likely in preparation for the planned addition of voice chat in League of Legends.

What do you make of these changes Riot has made? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

And there’s more to come as alleged footage of Locke gameplay leaked recently.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 7.5 Introduces New PvE Mode: Blood Hunt
item.content:encoded:
Marvel Rivals is getting a new PvE mode with season 7.5, with Blood Hunt arriving on April 23, 2026.

Netease is all set to release the upcoming seasonal update for Marvel Rivals on April 17, 2026. The developers will continue to provide players with new content as the season progresses, with the first new arrival being Black Cat. Just a few days later, players will receive a PvE game mode, with one more arriving towards the end of the month.

Marvel Rivals PvE Mode Features a Brand New Storyline and Four Epic Boss Battles, and More

Netease revealed the brand-new updates for season 7.5 in their latest dev diary video, which includes new information on the Blood Hunt mode for Marvel Rivals. Creative director Guangguang mentioned that the upcoming game mode will feature an all-new storyline, four epic boss battles, a new hero progression & trait system, and a brand-new loot system.

On the same day, we will see the release of the Hellfire Gala event, which will feature new themed costumes and cosmetics for Magik, Gambit, Moon Knight, Emma Frost, and Phoenix. Additionally, the winner of last year’s community vote, Emma Frost – Gala Star costume, will be given away for free to players.

Lastly, there’s another Marvel Rivals event kicking off this month: Path to Doomsday. Phase 1 of this event will begin on April 30 with a new game mode, Path to Doomsday: The Avengers mode. The upcoming game mode will allow players to experience the events of the Infinity War saga in Marvel Rivals by engaging against Loki in asymmetrical combat. The Times Square map will also feature a series of events.

In other news, Marvel Rivals aims to be an extension of the Marvel Cinematic Universe and a comprehensive, ongoing anime series. What are your thoughts on Marvel Rivals new PvE game mode, Blood Hunt? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the GTA leak raising Take-Two’s stock value by over $1 billion. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
